title: 11 Herbal Essential Oils For Shingles
intro_desc: If you're experiencing the discomfort of shingles, you may want to consider using herbal essential oils to help alleviate the symptoms. Shingles is a painful rash caused by the reactivation of the chickenpox virus, and it can be really tough to deal with. Herbal teas, which contain essential oils from plants like Zingiber officinale (ginger), Lavandula angustifolia (lavender), and Eucalyptus globulus (eucalyptus), have been found to provide relief from the pain and discomfort associated with shingles.  Ginger, for example, has anti-inflammatory properties that can help to reduce swelling and ease pain. It also has antibacterial properties that can help to prevent infection in the affected area. Lavender, on the other hand, is known for its calming effects and can help to reduce stress and promote relaxation. Eucalyptus, meanwhile, has decongestant properties that can help to relieve congestion and coughs that often accompany shingles.  Using herbal essential oils can bring a range of benefits to your life. For one, they can help to reduce the severity and duration of shingles symptoms, making it easier to manage your condition. They can also help to promote relaxation and reduce stress, which can be really helpful when you're feeling unwell. Additionally, herbal essential oils can be a natural and non-invasive way to manage your symptoms, which can be especially appealing if you're looking for an alternative to medication. By incorporating herbal essential oils into your treatment plan, you may find that you're able to manage your shingles symptoms more effectively and get back to your normal routine sooner.

remedies.remedy_desc: Zingiber officinale essential oils contains compounds like gingerol and shogaol, which have anti-inflammatory properties. These properties help reduce pain and swelling associated with shingles. The essential oil also contains sesquiterpenes, like bisabolol, which have antiviral properties that may help combat the herpes zoster virus that causes shingles. The warming and analgesic properties of Zingiber officinale essential oils, due in part to its gingerol content, can also help alleviate the burning and itching sensations common with shingles. By reducing inflammation and pain, and potentially inhibiting the virus, Zingiber officinale essential oils may provide relief for individuals suffering from shingles.
remedies.image_alt: Zingiber officinale herbal essential oils for shingles
remedy_procedure: Gather 2 cups of fresh Zingiber officinale roots and wash them with water.
Dry the roots in a single layer on a paper towel for 24 hours.
Use a food processor to grind 1 cup of the dried roots into a fine powder.
Combine the powder with 2 cups of carrier oil (like coconut or olive oil) in a glass jar.
Steep the mixture in a warm, dark place for 2 weeks, shaking the jar every day.

remedies.remedy_desc: Lavandula angustifolia essential oils contains a high concentration of linalool and linalyl acetate, two bioactive constituents that have anti-inflammatory and antiviral properties. These properties can help to reduce the severity and duration of shingles symptoms. Linalool, in particular, has been shown to inhibit the replication of the varicella-zoster virus, which causes shingles. The antiviral and anti-inflammatory properties of Lavandula angustifolia essential oils may also help to reduce pain and discomfort associated with shingles. When applied topically, the essential oil can also help to soothe and calm irritated skin, promoting a faster recovery from shingles.
remedies.image_alt: Lavandula angustifolia herbal essential oils for shingles
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of dried Lavandula angustifolia flowers. Use organic and high-quality flowers for best results.
Place the flowers in a clean glass jar and cover them with 2 cups of carrier oil like coconut or olive oil. Stir well.
Let the mixture sit in a dark place for 2-3 weeks, shaking the jar every day. This allows the oil to extract from the flowers.
After 2-3 weeks, strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass jar. Discard the solids.
Store the extracted Lavandula angustifolia essential oil in a cool, dark place. Use 5-7 drops in a carrier oil or bath water for relief from shingles symptoms.

remedies.remedy_desc: Eucalyptus globulus essential oils contains a compound called cineole, which has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce pain and discomfort associated with shingles. The oil also contains eudesmol, a compound that has antimicrobial properties, which may help prevent the spread of the shingles virus. Eucalyptus globulus essential oils has a decongestant property, which can help relieve the itchy and painful rash caused by shingles. The menthol content in the oil also helps to reduce pain and promote a sense of calm, making it easier to manage the symptoms of shingles. By applying topically, the oil can help to reduce the severity of shingles symptoms and promote a faster recovery.
remedies.image_alt: Eucalyptus globulus herbal essential oils for shingles
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of Eucalyptus globulus leaves and 2 cups of water in a large pot.
Heat the water over low heat for 10-15 minutes. Then, add the Eucalyptus globulus leaves.
Reduce heat to low and simmer for 30-40 minutes. Monitor the mixture to avoid boiling.
Remove the pot from heat and let it cool for 10 minutes. Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth into a bowl.
Transfer the extracted oil to a dark glass bottle using a funnel. Store in a cool, dark place.

remedies.remedy_desc: Echinacea purpurea essential oils contains active constituents like alkylamides, caffeic acid, and rosmarinic acid. These constituents have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that can help reduce the severity and duration of shingles symptoms. The alkylamides in Echinacea purpurea essential oils have been shown to inhibit the production of pro-inflammatory chemicals, which can exacerbate shingles pain. Additionally, the antioxidant properties of caffeic and rosmarinic acid can help reduce skin damage and promote wound healing in shingles patients. By using Echinacea purpurea essential oils, individuals may experience relief from shingles symptoms, such as pain, itching, and skin lesions.
remedies.image_alt: Echinacea purpurea herbal essential oils for shingles
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of Echinacea purpurea flowers, 1 cup of carrier oil (like jojoba or sweet almond oil), and a clean glass jar.
Fill a saucepan with 2 cups of water and bring it to a boil. Reduce heat to medium-low.
Add 2 tablespoons of Echinacea purpurea flowers to the saucepan and let it steep for 2-3 hours. Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth.
Combine the infused liquid with 1 cup of carrier oil in the glass jar. Stir well and let it sit in a cool, dark place for 2-3 days.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth again and discard the solids. Transfer the Echinacea purpurea essential oil to a small bottle and store it in a cool, dark place.

remedies.remedy_desc: Hypericum perforatum essential oils contains naringenin, quercetin, and rutin as its bioactive constituents. These compounds have potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help to reduce the severity of shingles symptoms. The antiviral properties of hyperforin and hypericin in Hypericum perforatum essential oils can inhibit the replication of the varicella-zoster virus that causes shingles. The oil's anti-inflammatory properties can also help to reduce pain and discomfort associated with shingles, while its antioxidant properties can promote wound healing and tissue repair. The combination of these properties makes Hypericum perforatum essential oils a potential natural remedy for shingles.
remedies.image_alt: Hypericum perforatum herbal essential oils for shingles
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh Hypericum perforatum flowers, 2 cups of carrier oil (like jojoba or sweet almond oil), and a clean glass jar with a lid.
Combine 1 cup of flowers with 2 cups of carrier oil in the glass jar. Stir well.
Store the jar in a cool, dark place for 2-3 weeks, shaking the jar every day to help the flowers release their oil.
After 2-3 weeks, strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass jar. Discard the solids.
Transfer the extracted oil to a dark glass bottle and label it. Use 5-7 drops of the oil per application to help with shingles symptoms.

remedies.remedy_desc: Melaleuca alternifolia essential oils contains compounds like cineole, limonene, and terpinen-4-ol, which have antiviral and anti-inflammatory properties. These properties can help reduce the severity and duration of shingles symptoms. The antiviral properties of terpinen-4-ol can inhibit the replication of the varicella-zoster virus, which causes shingles. The anti-inflammatory properties of cineole and limonene can reduce pain and swelling associated with shingles. By reducing the viral load and inflammation, Melaleuca alternifolia essential oils may provide relief from shingles symptoms.
remedies.image_alt: Melaleuca alternifolia herbal essential oils for shingles
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of Melaleuca alternifolia leaves, fresh or dried.
Combine the leaves with 2 cups of water in a saucepan and bring to a boil.
Reduce heat, cover, and simmer for 30-40 minutes.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth into a clean bowl, discarding the solids.
Allow the liquid to cool and then transfer it to a dark glass bottle for storage.

remedies.remedy_desc: Curcuma longa essential oils contains bioactive constituents like curcumin, demethoxycurcumin, and bisdemethoxycurcumin, which have anti-inflammatory and antiviral properties. These properties help reduce the pain and inflammation associated with shingles, a viral infection caused by the varicella-zoster virus. Curcumin, in particular, has potent antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects, which can help alleviate the discomfort and pain of shingles. The antiviral properties of Curcuma longa essential oils may also help reduce the severity and duration of shingles outbreaks. By reducing inflammation and pain, Curcuma longa essential oils may provide relief for individuals suffering from shingles.
remedies.image_alt: Curcuma longa herbal essential oils for shingles
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of Curcuma longa root and 2 cups of carrier oil (such as coconut or olive oil).
Wash the Curcuma longa root in cold water, then chop it into small pieces.
Combine the chopped Curcuma longa root with the carrier oil in a saucepan and heat it on low heat for 2 hours.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a bowl, and discard the solids.
Bottle the Curcuma longa essential oil and store it in a cool, dark place. Use 5-7 drops per application.

remedies.remedy_desc: Aloe vera essential oils contains boswellic acids, which have potent anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and pain associated with shingles. The essential oil also contains aloin, a compound that promotes wound healing and reduces the risk of infection. The anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties of aloe vera essential oils can help soothe itchy, blistering skin and promote the healing process. The presence of acemannan, a polysaccharide, can also stimulate the immune system to fight off the varicella-zoster virus that causes shingles. By reducing inflammation and promoting healing, aloe vera essential oils may help alleviate symptoms and support the body's natural recovery process.
remedies.image_alt: Aloe vera herbal essential oils for shingles
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of Aloe vera gel from a mature plant, and 2 cups of carrier oil (coconut or jojoba oil).
Transfer the Aloe vera gel to a blender or food processor and blend until smooth.
Add 1/2 teaspoon of vitamin E oil and blend well.
Combine the blended Aloe vera mixture with the carrier oil in a bowl and mix well.
Transfer the mixture to a glass bottle and store in a cool, dark place. Apply topically to affected areas 2-3 times a day.

remedies.remedy_desc: Ginkgo biloba essential oils contains flavoglycosides, bilobalide, and ginkgolides as its active constituents. These compounds have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce the severity of shingles symptoms. The flavoglycosides in Ginkgo biloba essential oils have antioxidant properties, protecting the skin from oxidative stress and promoting healing. The bilobalide and ginkgolides in Ginkgo biloba essential oils have also been shown to improve blood flow and reduce pain, making it a potential natural remedy for shingles. By using Ginkgo biloba essential oils topically, individuals with shingles may experience relief from inflammation, pain, and other symptoms.
remedies.image_alt: Ginkgo biloba herbal essential oils for shingles
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh Ginkgo biloba leaves and 1 cup of carrier oil (coconut or jojoba oil).
Clean and dry the Ginkgo biloba leaves, then chop them into small pieces.
Combine the chopped Ginkgo biloba leaves with 1 cup of carrier oil in a clean glass jar.
Steep the mixture in a cool, dark place for 2-3 weeks, shaking the jar every day. Strain the oil after the steeping period.
Filter the oil through a coffee filter or cheesecloth to remove any remaining plant particles. Store the Ginkgo biloba essential oil in a dark glass bottle.

remedies.remedy_desc: Silybum marianum essential oils contains a compound called silymarin, which is rich in bioactive constituents like flavonoids, alkaloids, and polyphenols. These constituents have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help to reduce pain and itching associated with shingles. Silymarin also has antiviral properties, which can help to fight the varicella-zoster virus that causes shingles. The essential oil's flavonoids, specifically isoorientin and isovitexin, have been shown to inhibit the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes, further reducing inflammation and discomfort. By using Silybum marianum essential oils, individuals may experience relief from shingles symptoms due to its anti-inflammatory and antiviral properties.
remedies.image_alt: Silybum marianum herbal essential oils for shingles
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of dried Silybum marianum flowers and 1 cup of carrier oil (like jojoba or sweet almond oil).
Combine the dried flowers and carrier oil in a clean glass jar. Stir well to coat the flowers evenly.
Store the jar in a cool, dark place for 2-3 weeks, shaking it daily. This allows the oil to extract from the flowers.
After 2-3 weeks, strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into another clean glass jar. Discard the solids.
Bottle the extracted oil in a dark glass container and label it. Use 5-10 drops of the oil in a carrier oil for topical application, as needed.

remedies.remedy_desc: Aloe barbadensis essential oils contains several bioactive constituents, including aloin, aloe-emodin, and acemannan, which have anti-inflammatory and antiviral properties. These properties help to reduce the severity and duration of shingles symptoms, such as pain and itching. The antiviral activity of acemannan and aloe-emodin can also help to shorten the duration of the shingles rash. Additionally, the anti-inflammatory properties of aloin and aloe-emodin can reduce swelling and redness associated with shingles. By reducing inflammation and viral replication, aloe barbadensis essential oils may provide relief from shingles symptoms.
remedies.image_alt: Aloe barbadensis herbal essential oils for shingles
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of Aloe barbadensis leaves, 2 cups of water, and a blender.
Blend the Aloe leaves with the water in the blender until smooth.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a bowl.
Add 1 teaspoon of a carrier oil (like coconut or olive oil) to 1 tablespoon of the strained Aloe mixture.
Mix well and store the Aloe essential oil in a dark glass bottle in the fridge for up to 5 days.

faq_prevent: Drinking herbal tea may help reduce stress and boost the immune system, but there's no direct evidence it can prevent shingles from forming. Some herbs like calendula and echinacea are believed to have antiviral properties, but more research is needed to confirm their effectiveness in preventing shingles.
faq_safe: Herbal teas can be a helpful addition to your shingles treatment, but it's essential to be cautious with daily consumption. Some teas, like echinacea and sage, may have side effects or interact with medications. It's best to start with small amounts and monitor how your body reacts. This will help you determine a safe and effective dose.
faq_timeline: Herbal teas may help with shingles symptoms, but it's hard to predict exactly when they'll kick in. Some people find relief within a few days, while others may take a week or two to notice a difference. It really depends on the individual and the specific herbal tea being used.
faq_time: For shingles, it's a good idea to drink herbal tea in the morning. This can help your body start the day feeling a bit better. Some people find that it eases their symptoms and gives them a bit more energy to tackle the day ahead.
faq_interaction: Herbal teas can interact with prescription medications for shingles. Certain herbs like St. John's Wort and ginkgo biloba may reduce the effectiveness of antiviral medications. Others like feverfew and valerian root might slow down healing. Drinking herbal teas while taking prescription medications can change how your body responds to treatment.
faq_pregnant: If you're pregnant and experiencing shingles, it's best to be cautious when drinking herbal teas. Some herbal teas, like calendula and peppermint, are often considered safe during pregnancy, but others may not be. Licorice root and wild yam, for example, can stimulate the uterus and cause complications. Always check the ingredients carefully before consuming.
faq_breastfeeding: When breastfeeding with shingles, it's best to be cautious with herbal teas. Some herbs can pass into breast milk, potentially affecting your baby. For example, ginseng and peppermint may be safe, but others like comfrey, goldenseal, and licorice root might not be. If you're unsure, you can try peppermint or ginseng tea, but check the ingredients and look for safe, breastfeeding-friendly options.
title_2: Cancer Patients with Shingles: 11 Herbal Essential Oils to Try
